title: epiphyllum hookeri subsp. pittieri
intro: Epiphyllum hookeri subsp. pittieri, also known as the Epiphyllum pittieri, is a species of flowering cactus native to the tropical forests of Central America. It is a type of epiphyte, a plant that grows on other plants, typically trees, without deriving nutrients from them. The Epiphyllum hookeri subsp. pittieri plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to have various health benefits such as reducing fever and soothing skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
